etymology.description: 電(電) (diàn, "electric") + 影(影) (yǐng, "shadow") forms 電影(電影) (diànyǐng, "film; movie") as cinematic projections are essentially 'electric shadows'. 明(明) (míng, "bright; clear") + 星(星) (xīng, "star") form 明星(明星) (míngxīng, "star; celebrity") indicating a 'bright star', symbolizing fame.
etymology.interpretation: 電影(電影) (diànyǐng, "film; movie") suggests a movie as a 'play of shadows' that is powered by electricity, combining visual and technological elements for a film. 明星(明星) (míngxīng, "star; celebrity") draws on the image of a 'bright star' in the sky, a guiding point—which in the celebrity context refers to someone prominent and widely admired. Together, 電影明星(電影明星) (diànyǐng míngxīng, "movie star") combines these senses to refer to a highly recognizable and admired figure within the cinematic world.

examples.chinese: 那个电影明星非常有名(那個電影明星非常有名)。
examples.english: That movie star is very famous.

examples.chinese: 电影明星们经常在大型活动中出现(電影明星們經常在大型活動中出現)。
examples.pinyin: Diànyǐng míngxīngmen jīngcháng zài dàxíng huódòng zhōng chūxiàn.
examples.english: Movie stars often appear at large events.

examples.chinese: 我最喜欢的电影明星刚获得了一个大奖(我最喜歡的電影明星剛獲得了一個大獎)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zuì xǐhuān de diànyǐng míngxīng gāng huòdéle yīgè dà jiǎng.
examples.english: My favorite movie star just won a big award.
